title: micrampelis gilensis
intro: Micrampelis gilensis, also known as Western teaberry or Western mountain tea, is a plant species belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America, particularly in the western United States and Canada. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to act as an astringent and anti-inflammatory, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. In herbal preparations, Micrampelis gilensis can be consumed as a tea or used in tinctures, demonstrating its potential health benefits such as relieving digestive issues and soothing respiratory problems.
